findings.title: Me() Handler Returns 200 OK on Permission Load Failure
findings.description: When GetUserPermissions() fails, the handler logs a WARNING and continues, returning 200 OK with empty permissions array. User sees themselves as having no permissions without any indication that permission loading failed.
findings.user_impact: User is locked out of all actions with no indication of why. Support receives reports of 'permission denied' with no clear cause.
findings.required_fix: Return 500 Internal Server Error when permission load fails. Log at ERROR level with user_id and tenant_id context.

findings.title: useCurrentUser() Hook Has No Retry, No Error Details
findings.description: The useCurrentUser hook throws generic error with no details and retry: false, causing transient network failures to become permanent. No logging of HTTP status codes or error context.
findings.user_impact: User sees loading screen indefinitely on network hiccup. Refreshing page doesn't help. No indication what's wrong (network, server, token expired, etc).
findings.required_fix: Add retry logic for transient errors (not 401/403). Log full HTTP details including status text and response snippet. Distinguish permanent vs temporary failures.

findings.title: AuthProvider Falls Back to Admin devUser on Auth Failure
findings.description: When useCurrentUser() fails, AuthProvider silently uses devUser with wildcard admin permissions. Violates CLAUDE.md: 'Mock/fake implementations belong only in tests'.
findings.user_impact: User with no permissions sees full admin UI and thinks they have admin access. Operations fail silently on backend with 403 responses that frontend doesn't handle.
findings.required_fix: Remove devUser fallback. Show error screen with retry button when auth fails. Never give users admin access on auth failure.

findings.title: 403 Middleware Swallows Request Context
findings.description: The forbiddenInterceptor catches all 403 responses and shows generic 'Access denied' toast. No context about which operation failed, which endpoint, what resource.
hidden_errors: Backend permission evaluator crash (returns 403 by default)
findings.user_impact: User sees generic 'Access denied' without knowing which operation failed. Toast disappears before user can read it. Support cannot correlate frontend error to backend logs.
findings.required_fix: Remove global 403 middleware. Let individual API call handlers show operation-specific errors with full context (method, URL, timestamp). Add error IDs and trace IDs for debugging.

findings.title: checkPermission() Implicitly Allows on Undefined Permissions
findings.description: When permissions is undefined, checkPermission returns true (allow all). Not justified and contradicts CLAUDE.md principle of explicit fallbacks.
hidden_errors: Missing permissions field in user response
findings.user_impact: If backend returns invalid response (missing permissions), user gets full access without realizing permission system is broken.
findings.required_fix: Change: if (permissions === undefined) return false; with console.warn. Undefined should mean 'permission unknown, deny by default'.

findings.title: JWT Profile Parsing Failures Logged But Response Incomplete
findings.description: When JWT parsing fails for profile claims, the handler logs WARNING but continues, returning user with missing name/email/preferred_username. User sees incomplete profile.
hidden_errors: JWT signature validation failure in middleware
findings.user_impact: User's profile shows name=null, email=null. Frontend displays empty fields. User confused about why profile is incomplete.
findings.required_fix: Include failure details in response: {profileFailure: 'failed to parse JWT'}. Log at ERROR level with trace ID for debugging.

findings.title: No Error IDs from errorIds.ts for Sentry Tracking
findings.description: All RBAC-related errors are logged but do not include error IDs from constants/errorIds.ts, making Sentry correlation impossible.

code_context.snippet:
slog.WarnContext(r.Context(), "sso me: failed to load user permissions", "error", err)
// Missing: "error_id", errorIds.RBACPermissionLoadFailed,

hidden_errors: Correlation between frontend and backend logs
Grouping errors by category in Sentry
findings.user_impact: Support cannot group permission-related failures by type. Operator sees 100s of individual errors instead of 'Permission load failing 50 times/hour'.
findings.required_fix: Add error IDs to all error logs: 'error_id', errorIds.RBACPermissionLoadFailed. Ensure errorIds.ts has constants for all RBAC failure modes.

findings.title: useCan() Called Without Null Safety in 35+ Page Files
findings.description: Pages call useCan() without null checks. If AuthProvider encounters error before page renders, useAuth() throws 'must be used within AuthProvider' error.

code_context.snippet:
const can = useCan();  // Throws if not in AuthProvider
disabled={!can('endpoints', 'create')}

hidden_errors: Auth error occurs before page setup
findings.user_impact: White screen of death if auth fails while page is rendering. No error boundary to catch and display readable error message.
findings.required_fix: Add error boundary wrapping all routes that use useAuth/useCan. Provide readable error screen with retry option.

recommendation: DO NOT MERGE without fixes. All 5 CRITICAL issues must be resolved before this code reaches production. These are silent failures that will cause user confusion, support escalations, and difficult-to-debug incidents.
claude_md_violations: No silent failures in production code
Mock/fake implementations belong only in tests
Fallback must be explicit and justified
Every error message must be actionable
